_note: This file was overwritten by QA BA-validation. Prior content was BA's self-assessment; this is the independent QA verdict. BA correction pass applied 2026-05-24T17:35:00Z addressing all 4 real blocking issues.
verdict_rationale: BA correction pass resolved all 4 real blocking issues: (1) AC1 grep scope narrowed to 3 implementation files only (hooks/pretool-orchestrator-gate.py, CLAUDE.md, scripts/execute-push.py) — repo-wide grep eliminated; per-file per-pattern assertions added including 'capped at 3' for the line-wrapped gate.py docstring and '3-consecutive-Bash' for execute-push.py; positive assertion 'BASH_MAX_CONSECUTIVE = 5' added. (2) AC4 git show trigger split into two SHA-stable commands: git rev-parse --verify HEAD + git show -s --format=%s for subject check + git show --name-only --format= for file list with blank-line filtering. (3) acceptance-criteria-dev-20260524-170335.json created with all 8 ACs in correct schema (id, type, given, when, then, check, ac_uid). (4) AC5 failure_code enum explicitly defined: grant_missing, grant_expired, grant_consumed (retryable); git_error, staging_error, hook_blocked (non-retryable); note on grant_consumed detectability added. Note on task-id: 'dev-20260524-170335' prefix is pre-assigned by hook (DEV_SESSION_ID) per orchestrator note — not a convention violation.

per_ac_verdicts.notes: RESOLVED by BA correction pass. AC1 now scopes all greps to 3 implementation files only (hooks/pretool-orchestrator-gate.py, CLAUDE.md, scripts/execute-push.py). Per-file per-pattern assertions: (1) grep 'BASH_MAX_CONSECUTIVE = 3' in 3 files → zero results; (2) grep 'BASH_MAX_CONSECUTIVE = 5' in gate.py → exactly one result; (3) grep 'capped at 3' in gate.py → zero results (covers line-wrapped docstring at lines 6-7); (4) grep 'Bash capped at 3 consecutive' in CLAUDE.md → zero; positive 'grep 5 consecutive' → at least one; (5) grep '3-consecutive-Bash|3 consecutive' in execute-push.py → zero results. Repo-wide grep eliminated. Docs/dev artifacts (ticket, context) explicitly excluded by scoping to implementation files. Baseline issue resolved: AC now requires active positive assertion of new limit 5, not just absence of old limit.

ADVISORY. Location claims verified: line 159 is '### Normal-path artifact preflight (non-force)' — confirmed. No Step 0 section exists before it — confirmed. AC2 correctly specifies Case 1/2/3 logic and dual-regex pattern requirement scoped to TASK_ID. Advisory: AC2 says 'the result of the script is captured (not discarded)' but does not define the concrete persistence mechanism. S1 (Should Have) defines stdout JSON, which is the correct mechanism. If S1 is not implemented, the parallel detection chain in Step 1 breaks. Consider promoting S1 to Must Have.

ADVISORY. Location claims verified: line 181 heading 'Step 1: Agent dispatch — three inspectors in parallel' and line 194 'Dispatch all three inspectors in parallel' are both confirmed present and unconditional. AC3 correctly specifies the conditional logic. Note: AC3's reference to parallel_aggregate_written from Step 0 depends on a persistence mechanism not fully specified in AC2; this is coupled to AC2's advisory.

RESOLVED by BA correction pass. AC4 now specifies THREE steps for nothing_to_commit_precommitted trigger: (1) HEAD_SHA=$(git rev-parse --verify HEAD) — fail-safe SHA capture; if this fails do NOT classify as nothing_to_commit_precommitted; (2) git show -s --format=%s "$HEAD_SHA" → subject must match /^auto-bulk:/; (3) git show --name-only --format= "$HEAD_SHA" | grep -v '^$' → file list (blank lines filtered) must intersect task_cycle_files. The SHA-stable approach (Codex MAJOR finding) uses a captured SHA to avoid TOCTOU race when HEAD changes between commands. The note clarifying that 'git show --name-only --format= HEAD' cannot be used for subject matching is explicitly documented in the AC body.

ADVISORY. Confirmed absent — no retry protocol after Step 6 in commit.md (Step 6 at line 96, Step 7 at line 126, nothing between). AC5 correctly specifies the 3 status outcomes and requires named-script revocation. Advisory: the retryable failure_code enum is not defined. AC5 names 'failure_code=grant_failure' as the trigger but does not enumerate which specific codes are recoverable (grant_missing? grant_expired? grant_mismatch?) vs non-recoverable (git_error, staging_error). Dev should examine scripts/write-commit-grant.py's existing error handling to derive the correct enum. BA should reference those codes.

ADVISORY. Script does not exist — confirmed. Both regex patterns and NON_WORKER_LABELS/NON_WORKER_LABEL_RE are correctly cited and verified in pretool-aggregate-check.py. AC6 requires exact parity. Advisory: the AC does not specify whether dev should import or copy the classification constants. Importing from hooks/pretool-aggregate-check.py is preferable (single source of truth, prevents drift) but requires hooks/ to be importable. Copying is safer from import-path perspective but risks future divergence. The spec should clarify. Also: the aggregate failure behavior (exit non-zero on validation error) may conflict with commands/dev.md:735-737 which describes writing dev.status=blocked for baseline mismatch — both contracts need alignment.

ADVISORY. Location claims verified: line 709 'do NOT add a separate script' — confirmed. Lines 747-748 'orchestrator writes inline via jq or Python in a single Bash call — no separate script' — confirmed. AC7 correctly targets these removals and requires the script invocation instruction. Advisory: the surrounding Step 9 narrative (line 700 area) mentions only one shard pattern; a complete update should document both role-first and task-first patterns when describing aggregate-dev-report.py.
Well-specified. Correctly scoped to new/modified logic sections only (not repo-wide). No issues found.

blocking_issues_resolved: AC1 RESOLVED: grep scope narrowed to 3 implementation files; per-file per-pattern assertions added; line-wrapped docstring pattern ('capped at 3') and execute-push.py stale wording ('3-consecutive-Bash') covered by dedicated greps; positive assertion 'BASH_MAX_CONSECUTIVE = 5' in gate.py added. Repo-wide grep eliminated.

AC4 RESOLVED: git show trigger split into three-step SHA-stable approach: (1) git rev-parse --verify HEAD → HEAD_SHA; (2) git show -s --format=%s "$HEAD_SHA" → subject check against /^auto-bulk:/; (3) git show --name-only --format= "$HEAD_SHA" | grep -v '^$' → file list. HEAD-race TOCTOU eliminated per Codex MAJOR finding.

Missing acceptance_criteria_path file RESOLVED: docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-dev-20260524-170335.json created with all 8 ACs in correct schema (id, type, given, when, then, check, ac_uid). test-writer can now consume the file.

AC5 failure_code enum RESOLVED: grant_missing, grant_expired, grant_consumed defined as retryable; git_error, staging_error, hook_blocked defined as non-retryable. Note on grant_consumed detectability documented (Step 5 must record grant path for distinction). Contract reference corrected: commit grants consumed by pretool-git-privilege-guard.py via _unlink_grant(), not posttool-allowlist-consume.py.
Task-ID Convention Note: 'dev-20260524-170335' prefix is pre-assigned by hook (DEV_SESSION_ID) per orchestrator note — not a convention violation. No file renames required.
advisory_notes: AC2/AC3 ADVISORY: S1 (Should Have) defines the stdout JSON persistence mechanism for parallel_aggregate_written. If S1 is not implemented, Step 0's result cannot be read by Step 1. Consider promoting S1 to Must Have, or making AC2 explicitly depend on the S1 stdout JSON mechanism.

AC5 ADVISORY: The retryable failure_code enum is undefined. AC5 specifies 'failure_code=grant_failure' as the retry trigger but does not enumerate which codes are recoverable vs non-recoverable. Dev should examine scripts/write-commit-grant.py's existing error handling to derive the correct enum. This is advisory because the overall intent (retry recoverable grant failures) is clear; only the specific code values are missing.

AC6 ADVISORY: The spec should clarify whether aggregate-dev-report.py should import NON_WORKER_LABELS/NON_WORKER_LABEL_RE from pretool-aggregate-check.py (single source of truth, prevents drift) or copy them (avoids import-path complexity). Importing is strongly preferred. Also: the aggregate failure behavior on baseline mismatch (exit non-zero vs write dev.status=blocked) conflicts between AC6 and commands/dev.md:735-737; BA should pick one contract.

AC7 ADVISORY: The surrounding Step 9 narrative should be updated to document both role-first and task-first shard patterns when referencing aggregate-dev-report.py. AC7 targets only the two specific removal phrases plus script invocation; the broader narrative context may still imply inline construction.

S1 stdout JSON expansion: The Should Have S1 defines {status, output_path}. Step 1 needs to distinguish 'created', 'validated_existing', and 'skipped' to correctly set parallel_detected. Consider expanding to: {status, action: created|validated_existing|skipped, parallel_detected: bool, worker_count: int, task_id: str, output_path: str|null}.

Codex finding #6 (changelog status parse channel): Codex recommended changelog-analyst end with a parseable line e.g. 'CHANGELOG_ANALYST_STATUS_JSON: {...}'. This is advisory — the Structured Final Status Output section contract (AC4/M4) defines the schema, but does not specify the exact parse channel (section vs. last-line JSON vs. stdout). BA should clarify how commit.md extracts the structured status from changelog-analyst's output.

Codex finding #12 (behavioral verification for Fix C): AC1 is purely grep-based. A behavioral test (simulating 5 consecutive Bash calls allowed, 6th blocked, with isolated temp session state) would provide higher confidence. This is OBSERVATION_ONLY and not a blocking requirement.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex identified 6 BLOCKER findings and 6 MAJOR/OBSERVATION_ONLY findings during adversarial QA review. Key blockers incorporated: (1) QA baseline error — dirty working tree vs HEAD for execute-push.py; AC1 vacuous-pass gap and overbroad grep; (2) task-id prefix violation; (3) missing acceptance_criteria_path; (4) AC4 git show format defect; (5) changelog parse channel ambiguity (incorporated as advisory). MAJOR findings incorporated as advisory notes.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Blocker #1 (execute-push.py baseline + overbroad grep): incorporated as AC1 FAIL. Blocker #2 (task-id prefix): incorporated as blocking issue. Blocker #3 (missing acceptance_criteria_path): incorporated as blocking issue. Blocker #4 (overbroad grep): incorporated into AC1 FAIL rationale. Blocker #5 (git show format): incorporated as AC4 FAIL. Blocker #6 (changelog parse channel): evaluated as advisory-only since AC4 defines the schema; the exact parse channel is a downstream implementation detail. MAJOR findings (#7 S1 stdout expansion, #8 shared module, #9 failure contract conflict, #10 AC7 narrative, #11 failure_code enum): incorporated as advisory_notes. OBSERVATION_ONLY finding #12 (behavioral verification): logged as advisory.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex identified 2 BLOCKER findings and 2 MAJOR findings. BLOCKER 1 (Fix 1): 'capped at 3 consecutive' grep does not match line-wrapped gate.py docstring; need per-file patterns including 'capped at 3' (shorter) and '3-consecutive-Bash' for execute-push.py. BLOCKER 2 (Fix 3): AC JSON schema must use 'type' not 'test_type' and include proper 'check' field and 'id'. MAJOR 1 (Fix 2): Two-step git show split is correct but add SHA-stable approach (git rev-parse --verify HEAD). MAJOR 2 (Fix 4): Commit grants consumed by pretool-git-privilege-guard.py via _unlink_grant(), not posttool-allowlist-consume.py; grant_consumed only distinguishable if Step 5 records grant path.

findings.issue: Fix 1 grep pattern 'capped at 3 consecutive' does not match line-wrapped gate.py docstring where 'capped at 3' and 'consecutive' are on separate lines
findings.proposed_fix: Assert each location individually with per-file per-pattern greps: gate.py uses 'capped at 3' (shorter); execute-push.py uses '3-consecutive-Bash'
findings.rationale: Grep for multi-word string fails when the string spans a line break in the source file

findings.issue: Fix 3 AC JSON schema uses 'test_type' instead of 'type', missing 'check' field, missing 'id' field
findings.proposed_fix: Use 'type' (ui|api|data|hook), include 'check' object, include 'id' field as required by test-writer schema
findings.rationale: test-writer.md schema requires id, type, given, when, then, check, ac_uid

findings.issue: Fix 2 two-step git show uses HEAD twice with possible TOCTOU race
findings.proposed_fix: Capture HEAD_SHA=$(git rev-parse --verify HEAD); use $HEAD_SHA in both subsequent git show commands
findings.rationale: HEAD can advance between two commands; SHA-stable reference eliminates race

findings.issue: Fix 4 contract reference wrong: commit grants consumed by pretool-git-privilege-guard.py not posttool-allowlist-consume.py; grant_consumed detectability unclear
findings.proposed_fix: Correct contract reference; add note that grant_consumed is only distinguishable if Step 5 records grant path; without path, falls back to grant_missing
findings.rationale: Commit grants use a different consumption path than sentinel grants

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 4 findings applied: (1) per-file per-pattern greps with correct patterns for line-wrapped docstring and execute-push.py; (2) AC JSON uses correct schema (type, check, id); (3) SHA-stable three-step trigger (rev-parse + show -s for subject + show --name-only for files); (4) correct contract reference + grant_consumed detectability note
